6	Mappings in GO have been historically represented as xrefs
7

9	
10	Mappings are always represented at the term level. Mappings should not be confused with definition provance, although there
11	may be some duplication here, this is OK. Mappings should not be confused with external logical definition (we never map to CHEBI or UBERON for example, but we may have relationships or logical definitions pointing there).
12	
13	More recently in GO we have been concerned with the *meaning* of a mapping and newer mappings always have a skos predicate for the mapping indicated as a trailing qualifier.

[MetaCyc:RXN-22693, MetaCyc:RXN-22694]

31	xref: MetaCyc:RXN-22694 {source="skos:narrowMatch"}

file.content:
---
name: mapping
description: For working with mappings (dbxrefs) in GO.
---

Mappings in GO have been historically represented as xrefs

`xref: EC:2.5.1.30`

Mappings are always represented at the term level. Mappings should not be confused with definition provance, although there
may be some duplication here, this is OK. Mappings should not be confused with external logical definition (we never map to CHEBI or UBERON for example, but we may have relationships or logical definitions pointing there).

More recently in GO we have been concerned with the *meaning* of a mapping and newer mappings always have a skos predicate for the mapping indicated as a trailing qualifier.

```
id: GO:0000031
name: mannosylphosphate transferase activity
xref: MetaCyc:RXN-22693 {source="skos:narrowMatch"}
```

means that the GO ID has a narrower concept in the MetaCyc ID

Example where the external term is narrower in meaning:

```
id: GO:0000031
name: mannosylphosphate transferase activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: GDP-alpha-D-mannose + n {[alpha-D-Man-(1->2)-alpha-D-Man-(1->2)]-alpha-D-Man-(1->6)}60-(Man9GlcNAc2-[protein] = phosphorylated {[alpha-D-Man-(1->2)-alpha-D-Man-(1->2)]-alpha-D-Man-(1->6)}60-(Man9GlcNAc2-[protein] + n GMP + n H+ or GDP-alpha-D-mannose + alpha-D-Man-(1->3)-alpha-D-Man-(1->3)-alpha-D-Man-(1->2)-alpha-D-Man-(1->2)-alpha-D-Man-3-O-(Ser/Thr)-[protein] = alpha-D-Man-(1->3)-alpha-D-Man-(1->3)-[alpha-D-Man-6P-]-alpha-D-Man-(1->2)-alpha-D-Man-(1->2)-alpha-D-Man-3-O-(Ser/Thr)-[protein] + GMP + H+." [MetaCyc:RXN-22693, MetaCyc:RXN-22694]
xref: MetaCyc:RXN-22693 {source="skos:narrowMatch"}
xref: MetaCyc:RXN-22694 {source="skos:narrowMatch"}
is_a: GO:0016758 ! hexosyltransferase activity
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/28340" xsd:anyURI
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/28811" xsd:anyURI
```

Example where the RHEA ID is equivalent, we disambiguate two MetaCycs and the EC is broader

```
id: GO:0000121
name: sn-glycerol 1-phosphatase activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "Catalysis of the reaction: H2O + sn-glycerol 1-phosphate = glycerol + phosphate." [RHEA:46084]
synonym: "alpha-glycerol phosphatase activity" RELATED [EC:3.1.3.21]
synonym: "alpha-glycerophosphatase activity" RELATED [EC:3.1.3.21]
synonym: "glycerol 3-phosphatase activity" RELATED [EC:3.1.3.21]
synonym: "glycerol 3-phosphate phosphohydrolase activity" RELATED [EC:3.1.3.21]
synonym: "glycerol-1-phosphatase activity" BROAD [EC:3.1.3.21]
synonym: "glycerol-1-phosphate phosphohydrolase activity" RELATED [EC:3.1.3.21]
synonym: "glycerol-3-phosphate phosphatase activity" RELATED [EC:3.1.3.21]
xref: EC:3.1.3.21 {source="skos:broadMatch"}
xref: MetaCyc:GLYCEROL-1-PHOSPHATASE-RXN {source="skos:broadMatch"}
xref: MetaCyc:RXN-14964 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
xref: RHEA:46084 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
is_a: GO:0016791 ! phosphatase activity
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/27964" xsd:anyURI
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/28176" xsd:anyURI
```

Note that the GO def follows the RHEA definition precisely, and one of the participants is 1-phosphate; there is a similar reaction (RHEA:66372; sn-glycerol 3-phosphate + H2O = glycerol + phosphate) with a different participant. Both are mapped to the broader EC.

## Looking up external IDs (EC and RHEA)

`runoak -i sqlite:obo:eccode info EC:3.1.3.21`
=> EC:3.1.3.21 ! glycerol-1-phosphatase

`runoak -i sqlite:obo:rhea info RHEA:46084`
=> RHEA:46084 ! sn-glycerol 1-phosphate + H2O = glycerol + phosphate

Always lookup these IDs.

## Special Rules for MF

Most mappings are in MF, and it is possible to be precise due to the fact that RHEA has precise participants. ALWAYS consider the reaction participants when mapping MFs.

# EC 1.1.1.358 Overview

**Enzyme Name:** The accepted name is "2-dehydropantolactone reductase."

**Reaction:** The enzyme catalyzes the following transformation: "(R)-pantolactone + NADP+ = 2-dehydropantolactone + NADPH + H+"

This enzyme participates in an alternative biosynthetic route for producing (R)-pantothenate, commonly known as vitamin B5. The entry specifically covers variants where the stereochemical specificity for NADP+ has not been fully characterized.

content.content:
# EC 1.17.3.2: Xanthine Oxidase

**Name:** The accepted name is "xanthine oxidase," also known as "xanthine:oxygen oxidoreductase" in its systematic designation.

**Reaction:** The enzyme catalyzes the following transformation: "xanthine + H2O + O2 = urate + H2O2"

This iron-molybdenum flavoprotein oxidizes xanthine to urate while producing hydrogen peroxide as a byproduct. The enzyme can also oxidize hypoxanthine and other purines under physiological conditions.

This iron-molybdenum flavoprotein oxidizes xanthine to urate while producing hydrogen peroxide as a byproduct. The enzyme can also oxidize hypoxanthine and other purines under physiological conditions.

content.content:
# EC 1.14.14.24: Vitamin D 25-Hydroxylase

**Accepted Name:** "vitamin D 25-hydroxylase"

**Reaction:** This enzyme catalyzes the conversion of calciol (vitamin D3) to calcidiol (25-hydroxyvitamin D3) using oxygen and a reduced electron donor. The process produces the hydroxylated form along with water as a byproduct.

This microsomal enzyme from liver tissue is responsible for the initial bioactivation step of vitamin D3 in humans, with CYP2R1 being the primary isoform functioning in the body.

input.content:
## Summary

Addresses geneontology/go-ontology#31962. Adds missing EC/RHEA cross-references on four oxidoreductase activity terms and renames one term to match EC nomenclature.

## Changes (all in `src/ontology/go-edit.obo`)

### GO:0036441 — 2-dehydropantolactone reductase activity
- Added `xref: EC:1.1.1.358 {source="skos:exactMatch"}`
- EC 1.1.1.358 is "2-dehydropantolactone reductase"; reaction `(R)-pantolactone + NADP+ = 2-dehydropantolactone + NADPH + H+` exactly matches the existing GO definition (and the already-present RHEA:18981).

### GO:0070675 — hypoxanthine oxidase activity
- Added `xref: EC:1.17.3.2 {source="skos:broadMatch"}`
- Added `xref: RHEA:68012 {source="skos:exactMatch"}`
- Changed definition xref from `[GOC:mah, GOC:pde]` to `[RHEA:68012]` (the reaction in the GO def matches RHEA:68012, `hypoxanthine + O2 + H2O = xanthine + H2O2`, verified via the RHEA RDF dump)
- EC:1.17.3.2 ("xanthine oxidase") covers both xanthine and hypoxanthine oxidation by the same enzyme, hence `broadMatch` rather than exactMatch.

### GO:0004855 — xanthine oxidase activity
- Changed `EC:1.17.3.2` mapping from `skos:exactMatch` → `skos:broadMatch` (same rationale as above: the EC class covers two reactions, the GO term covers only the xanthine → urate reaction).
- RHEA:21132 remains the `skos:exactMatch` for the xanthine → urate reaction.

### GO:0030343 — vitamin D 25-hydroxylase activity (was: vitamin D3 25-hydroxylase activity)
- Renamed term to match EC:1.14.14.24's accepted name ("vitamin D 25-hydroxylase") and the general GO naming pattern.
- Previous label `vitamin D3 25-hydroxylase activity` retained as an EXACT synonym to preserve search/recognition.
- Added `xref: EC:1.14.14.24 {source="skos:exactMatch"}`.

In all four cases a `term_tracker_item` pointing to issue #31962 was added (no `created_by` / `creation_date` changes, since these terms already existed).

## Verification

- Confirmed RHEA:68012 → EC:1.17.3.2 mapping and equation via the RHEA RDF dump (`https://ftp.expasy.org/databases/rhea/rdf/rhea.rdf.gz`): equation `hypoxanthine + O2 + H2O = xanthine + H2O2`; cited PMID:20615869, PMID:23116398.
- Confirmed EC names and reactions for 1.1.1.358, 1.17.3.2, and 1.14.14.24 against the IUBMB enzyme nomenclature site.

## Validation

- `robot verify` with the full standard SPARQL QC suite (equivalent-classes, trailing-whitespace, owldef-self-reference, synonym-label-match, replacedby-obsolete, replacedby-namespace, missing-namespace, duplicate-(exact-)synonym, non-IRI-value, non-anyURI-value, obsolete-definition, definition-constraints, one-to-one-xrefs-by-subject, one-to-one-xrefs-by-value, xref-syntax): **all pass, 0 violations**.
- `robot reason -r ELK -i go-edit.obo`: **completes without errors** (full `make travis_build` was not run because the local sandbox lacks parts of the build chain, but the syntactic and reasoning checks above cover the categories most likely to be perturbed by xref/label edits).

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN: Issue analyzed; intent (add EC/RHEA xrefs and one rename) is unambiguous; no clarification needed.
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION: SPARQL QC + ELK reasoning pass on the pristine file.
- [N/A] RESEARCH: Not required — issue specified the exact EC/RHEA IDs to add. External IDs cross-checked against IUBMB and RHEA's own RDF.
- [x] TERM-SEARCH: All four target terms located via `obo-grep.pl`; no name collisions (only the obsolete "obsolete vitamin D 25-hydroxylase activity" stanza shares the stem, which is expected).
- [N/A] DESIGN-PATTERNS: No new term construction or logical definition changes; pure metadata edits.
- [x] EDITS: Used `obo-checkout.pl` → edit in `terms/` → `obo-checkin.pl` workflow.
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS: Unchanged.
- [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS: /mapping skill consulted (skos predicates assigned per the broader-EC / exact-RHEA pattern; broadMatch chosen because EC:1.17.3.2 groups two distinct reactions).
- [x] METADATA: No `created_by`/`creation_date` touched on these legacy terms; added `term_tracker_item` for #31962.
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: robot SPARQL QC + ELK reasoning.
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION: No PMIDs introduced. EC/RHEA IDs verified against authoritative sources.
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED: Single commit on `src/ontology/go-edit.obo`.
